task_completion.description: Open the asymmetric rocket design in OpenRocket
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket application window is open, showing 'asymmetric_rocket.ork' loaded, with the 'Action Camera' visible in the component tree.
A Mass Component configuration window is open in OpenRocket, and the component name is explicitly set to 'Counterweight'.

task_completion.description: Configure the counterweight's mass, radial position, and angle to neutralize the camera's moment
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Counterweight configuration window shows Radial direction = 180 degrees, Radial position <= 19.0 mm, and Mass set such that Mass * Radial Position equals 1500 g*mm (e.g., 100g at 15mm, or 78.9g at 19mm).

task_completion.description: Verify stability with a flight simulation
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Flight simulations' tab in OpenRocket is visible, showing a completed simulation run with stable flight results (e.g., green checkmarks, calculated apogee/velocity).

task_completion.description: Save the balanced design to the correct path
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket title bar shows 'balanced_rocket.ork' or a save dialog is visible saving the file to '/home/ga/Documents/rockets/balanced_rocket.ork'.

task_completion.description: Document the counterweight configuration in a report
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing 'balance_report.txt' in '/home/ga/Documents/exports/' containing the counterweight's mass, radial position, and angle (180 degrees).

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenRocket GUI to modify the rocket
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the OpenRocket graphical interface (component tree, configuration windows) rather than directly editing the .ork XML file in a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent actually ran the flight simulation in the software
integrity.visual_evidence: The simulation results in OpenRocket show populated, realistic values (apogee, velocity, flight time) indicating the engine was executed, not just an empty or un-run simulation table.

integrity.description: The values in the report match the actual configuration applied in OpenRocket
integrity.visual_evidence: The mass, radial position, and angle written in 'balance_report.txt' exactly match the values entered into the OpenRocket Counterweight configuration window.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected moment to neutralize is 0.0015 kg*m (1500 g*mm). The counterweight must be placed at 180 degrees radial direction. Its mass (in grams) multiplied by its radial position (in mm) must equal exactly 1500 (e.g., 100g at 15mm, or 78.9g at 19mm). The task description states a max internal radius of 19.0 mm, but the actual model's internal radius is 11.5 mm; any radial position <= 19.0 mm is acceptable as long as the moment balances. The final balanced file must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/rockets/balanced_rocket.ork' and the report to '/home/ga/Documents/exports/balance_report.txt'.
